compiled_state.title: Enrich Planning Pipeline Context to Prevent LLM Agent Failures
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
The DSO planning pipeline (brainstorm, preplanning, implementation-plan, sprint) generates task descriptions for LLM sub-agents but lacks contextual signals that prevent common failures identified by academic research. Consumer chains are not included (agents modify shared files without knowing who imports them), pattern examples are not provided for unfamiliar code areas, research findings are discarded between skills (same tool researched 3 times across brainstorm to preplanning), and sub-agents have no retry budget (they can spiral indefinitely on difficult tasks, with LLM-behavioral failures dominating debug sessions).

## Success Criteria
- Implementation-plan generates enriched task descriptions: (a) Downstream Consumers section (structurally distinct heading separate from File Impact; discovered via ast-grep/fuzzy-match) for tasks modifying files with external consumers, (b) Pattern Reference section with code examples dynamically retrieved from codebase (capped at 30 lines) for tasks with low/medium pattern familiarity (complexity evaluator Dimension 7), (c) requires_visual_verification metadata with Playwright AC for tasks touching template/CSS/JS/component files
- Research findings compound across pipeline: brainstorm/feasibility findings persist in versioned researchFindings array in PREPLANNING_CONTEXT; preplanning merged post-decomposition research phase skips verified capabilities, light-verifies partially_verified, fully researches unverified/contradicted; empty array means no prior research (full verification runs)
- Sub-agent task descriptions include 3-cycle retry budget. Sequential escalation: sonnet (3 cycles) to opus with diagnostic context (3 cycles) to user with full history. Opus escalation deferred when global throttle reports MAX_AGENTS: 0.

## Scenario Analysis
- Pattern Reference examples may bloat context window. Mitigated: 30-line cap, dynamic retrieval.

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Extend task descriptions with contextual signals (consumers, patterns, visual verification) separated from conflict detection signals via distinct section headings. Compound research findings across skills via PREPLANNING_CONTEXT schema extension. Enforce retry budgets at orchestrator level with model escalation.

comments.body:
### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none (user-provided research: He et al. 2026, IEEE SDHD, EvalPlus/ACL, SWE-agent/Yang et al.)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 1 (of 16 red team findings; 1 applies to this epic)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: triggered — depth: 0 (scope-split from combined spec)
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Adversarial review: 9 findings total, 7 accepted, 2 rejected. Full exchange artifact path not resolved (blue team sub-agent returned template path only).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked — Agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue that could send implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
comments.author: Test
